prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline was computed using DFT at tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting) in the gas phase. The Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor were calculated via finite-difference charges from single-point calculations on the neutral, singly-oxidized, and singly-reduced species using Mulliken population analysis in PySCF. All three SCF calculations converged cleanly. The most electrophilic site is C3 (ortho carbon) with f⁺ = 2.298; the most nucleophilic site is N (amino) with f⁻ = 0.205. The charge-conservation integrity checks passed for both f⁺ and f⁻ (sums = 1.0). The Fukui indices correctly predict ortho/para reactivity activation by the electron-donating aniline group. No warnings were reported.
